chapter_question: How does the pseudosphere make hyperbolic geometry measurable?
inspection_targets: The tractrix surface and half-plane model encode the same negative curvature.
The tractrix construction explains the surface of revolution.
Euclidean size changes while hyperbolic radius stays fixed.
Optical geodesics meet the boundary orthogonally.
The models transfer distances through a conformal map.

source_span.focus: Uses the pseudosphere to connect tractrix geometry, constant negative curvature, and the Poincare models of hyperbolic geometry.
source_span.label: Chapter 05
source_span.title: The Pseudosphere and the Hyperbolic Plane
topics: The tractrix and construction of the pseudosphere
Angle of parallelism in hyperbolic geometry
